title: alnus serrulatoides var. katoana
intro: Alnus serrulatoides var. katoana, also known as Japanese alder, is a variety of alder tree native to Japan. It is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of Japanese alder include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating infections. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Japanese alder are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
